quiz_questions.question_text: Heat capacity is the amount of energy needed to:

options.text: Change the phase of an object
Raise the temperature of an object by 1°C

quiz_questions.explanation: Heat transfer occurs by conduction (contact), convection (fluid movement), and radiation (electromagnetic waves). Specific heat capacity (c) is the energy needed to raise 1 g by 1°C: q = mcΔT. The answer is 'Raise the temperature of an object by 1°C'. The other options — 'Dissolve a substance', 'Change the phase of an object', and 'Compress a gas' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

quiz_questions.question_text: Heat capacity differs from specific heat because it:

options.text: Depends on the total mass of the object

quiz_questions.explanation: Heat transfer occurs by conduction (contact), convection (fluid movement), and radiation (electromagnetic waves). Specific heat capacity (c) is the energy needed to raise 1 g by 1°C: q = mcΔT. The answer is 'Depends on the total mass of the object'. The other options — 'Is always the same', 'Is per gram', and 'Only applies to gases' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

quiz_questions.question_text: The units of heat capacity are:
quiz_questions.explanation: Heat transfer occurs by conduction (contact), convection (fluid movement), and radiation (electromagnetic waves). Specific heat capacity (c) is the energy needed to raise 1 g by 1°C: q = mcΔT. The answer is 'J/°C (or cal/°C)'. The other options — 'J/mol', 'J/g·°C', and '°C/J' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

quiz_questions.question_text: A large body of water has a high heat capacity because:

options.text: It has a large mass and high specific heat

quiz_questions.explanation: Heat transfer occurs by conduction (contact), convection (fluid movement), and radiation (electromagnetic waves). Specific heat capacity (c) is the energy needed to raise 1 g by 1°C: q = mcΔT. The answer is 'It has a large mass and high specific heat'. The other options — 'Water has low specific heat', 'It is always cold', and 'It evaporates easily' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

quiz_questions.question_text: Doubling the mass of a substance:
quiz_questions.explanation: Difference from Specific Heat : Heat capacity depends on total mass; specific heat is per gram. Relationship — C = m × c (heat capacity = mass × specific heat). The other options — 'Has no effect', 'Halves its heat capacity', and 'Triples it' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

Heat transfer occurs by conduction (contact), convection (fluid movement), and radiation (electromagnetic waves). Specific heat capacity (c) is the energy needed to raise 1 g by 1°C: q = mcΔT. The answer is 'Mass'. The other options — 'Pressure', 'Temperature', and 'Volume' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

quiz_questions.question_text: An object with high heat capacity:

options.text: Requires more energy to change its temperature

quiz_questions.explanation: Heat transfer occurs by conduction (contact), convection (fluid movement), and radiation (electromagnetic waves). Specific heat capacity (c) is the energy needed to raise 1 g by 1°C: q = mcΔT. The answer is 'Requires more energy to change its temperature'. The other options — 'Changes temperature easily', 'Is always a metal', and 'Has low mass' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.
